title: I am not skilled to understand
firstLine: I am not skilled to understand
publicDomainBasis: Dora Greenwell (1821–1882), William James Kirkpatrick (1838–1921). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
I am not skilled to understand
What God hath willed, what God hath planned;
I only know at His right hand
Stands One who is my Savior.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
I take Him at His word indeed:
“Christ died for sinners”—this I read;
For in my heart I find a need
Of Him to be my Savior!

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
That He should leave His place on high
And come for sinful man to die,
You count it strange? So once did I
Before I knew my Savior!

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
And, oh, that He fulfilled may see
The travail of His soul in me,
And with His work contented be,
As I with my dear Savior!

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
Yes, living, dying, let me bring
My strength, my solace from this spring—
That He who lives to be my King
Once died to be my Savior!
